Michael B. Gonzales

Research The Alpha Project

Biography

EDUCATION
Ph.D. Molecular Biology, Texas A&M University 1999
B.S. Molecular and Cell Biology, Texas A&M University 1993
 
PROFESSIONAL EXPERIENCE
Head of Research Informatics, Invitrogen Corp., Carlsbad, CA
March 2002 - August 2003
 
Director of Genomics, DoubleTwist Inc., Oakland, CA
February 2000 - March 2002

Postdoctoral Research Associate, Institute of Biosciences and Technology, Houston, TX
May 1999 - February 2000


CURRENT RESEARCH INTERESTS
I am interested in developing computational methods for the analysis and integration of molecular sequence and structure with other biological data that will allow us to more fully understand the biology of an organism.


SELECTED PUBLICATIONS
D. Janine Sherrier, Gail S. Taylor, Kevin A. T. Silverstein, Michael B. Gonzales and Kathryn A. VandenBosch
Accumulation of Extracellular Proteins Bearing Unique Proline-Rich Motifs in Intercellular Spaces of the Legume Nodule Parenchyma. In preparation.
 
Michael B. Gonzales, Aruna Myneni.
Comprehensive Analysis of the Mouse Transcriptome. Presented at the Genomic Sequencing and Analysis Conference, Miami, Florida. September 2000.
 
Jeremiah B. Frueauf1, Marti Dolata, Joseph F. Leykam, Elizabeth A. Lloyd, Michael B. Gonzales, Katherine VandenBosch and Marcia J. Kieliszewski
Peptides isolated from cell walls of Medicago truncatula nodules and uninfected root. Phytochemistry. 2000 Nov;55(5):429-38.
 
Keith L. Wycoff, Stephen Hunt, Michael B. Gonzales, Kathryn A. VandenBosch, David B. Layzell, and Ann M. Hirsch
Effects of Oxygen on Nodule Physiology and Expression of Nodulins in Alfalfa. Plant Physiol. 1998 117: 385-395.
 
Michael B. Gonzales and Kathryn A. VandenBosch. Proline-rich proteins and peroxidases in Medicago truncatula:
